title: Identifying counterfeit goods
wikitext:
[]: Identifying counterfeit goods             Quote Post by KaoShinForever » Sat Feb 26, 2022 3:37 am Hello, guys. I need some help regarding identifying offical vs unoffical goods. My friend told me that she once saw this post that says if the © mark of the merchandise is ©カラー then it is fake, and if the mark says ©khara. But I saw that goods from the official yahoo Japan store of evangelion all have ©カラー. Then the former (©カラー) one is real, right? cuz one of my friend believed that the ©khara is the real one so she only bought goods with that mark..... RIP...just in case...

[]: Re: Identifying counterfeit goods             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Sat Feb 26, 2022 8:28 am In Japan the studio name is often written in katakana, it is more convenient for them; you will see the "©khara" mark in the west (sometimes in Japan too). The picture below is an official product and has the copyright mark "©カラー" in it (source: EVANGELION STORE Yahoo! Shopping ): SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Identifying counterfeit goods             Quote Post by KaoShinForever » Sat Feb 26, 2022 8:31 am JoelcrNeto wrote: In Japan the studio name is often written in katakana, it is more convenient for them, you will see the "©khara" mark in the west (sometimes in Japan too). The picture below is an official product and has the copyright mark "©カラー" (source: EVANGELION STORE Yahoo! Shopping ): SPOILER: Show

[]: Identifying counterfeit goods             Quote Post by KaoShinForever » Sat Feb 26, 2022 3:37 am Hello, guys. I need some help regarding identifying offical vs unoffical goods. My friend told me that she once saw this post that says if the © mark of the merchandise is ©カラー then it is fake, and if the mark says ©khara. But I saw that goods from the official yahoo Japan store of evangelion all have ©カラー. Then the former (©カラー) one is real, right? cuz one of my friend believed that the ©khara is the real one so she only bought goods with that mark..... RIP...just in case...
